title: media.rs: stored mime_type mismatches actual file bytes (always PNG)
description: process_image_bytes() always re-encodes to PNG regardless of input format. The mime string from sniff_image_mime() is stored in the DB and used for the file extension, but the bytes written to disk are always PNG. A JPEG input gets a .jpg filename and image/jpeg in the DB, but the file contains PNG data. HTTP clients will reject this: the Content-Type header served for that media file will disagree with the magic bytes. Fix: either re-encode to the detected format, or always use image/png and .png regardless of input.
close_reason: All pass 4 fixes applied: media SSRF+size guard, mime/format consistency, proper keyId parsing, fail-closed sig verify, path reconstruction, key owner validation, media cap, blocking stdin fix, conflicts_with
